title: Cicuta douglasii
intro: Cicuta douglasii, commonly known as Douglas's hemlock or poison hemlock, is a highly toxic perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including gout, arthritis, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Cicuta douglasii include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. However, the most common herbal preparations made with this plant are not recommended, as the plant is highly toxic and can be fatal if ingested in large quantities; instead, some traditional herbalists use small amounts in specific, controlled preparations, such as tinctures or infusions, but these are not commonly used or recommended.
taxonomy: Cicuta douglasii is classified as a species within the genus Cicuta, which is part of the family Apiaceae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf shape and root structure. The genus Cicuta is commonly known as the water hemlock, and it contains several species found in North America. Cicuta douglasii is one of the species within this genus and is native to the western regions of North America. Its classification helps identify the plant's unique characteristics and its place within the plant kingdom.
uses: Cicuta douglasii, also known as Douglas' water hemlock, is a highly toxic plant and not used for medicinal purposes due to its high content of cicutoxin. The plant's toxic properties make it extremely hazardous to humans and animals, and any accidental ingestion can lead to severe health issues. In fact, cicutoxin is 1,000 times more potent than tetrodotoxin, a toxin found in some species of pufferfish. Due to its high toxicity, there is limited research on the potential medicinal uses of Cicuta douglasii. However, it's worth noting that some studies suggest the plant may have potential in the development of new pharmaceuticals, but this requires further research and caution.
properties: Cicuta douglasii, also known as Douglas's water hemlock, has a long history of use in traditional medicine by indigenous cultures in North America. Its roots and leaves are said to have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. However, it's essential to note that the therapeutic properties of Cicuta douglasii are not well-documented and its use can be highly toxic if not prepared correctly. Ingestion of the plant's toxic compounds can cause severe symptoms, including paralysis and death. Due to its potential toxicity, it's recommended to avoid using Cicuta douglasii for therapeutic purposes.
constituents: Cicuta douglasii, also known as Douglas's water hemlock, contains a variety of toxic compounds. The active constituents of this plant include alkaloids, such as cicutoxin and cicutol, which are responsible for its potent neurotoxic effects. These compounds can cause a range of symptoms, from muscle spasms and seizures to respiratory failure and death. The plant's toxicity is so severe that it is considered one of the most poisonous plants in North America. Ingestion of even small amounts of Douglas's water hemlock can be fatal.
parts: Cicuta douglasii, also known as Douglas' poison hemlock, is a highly toxic plant. The entire plant, including the roots, stems, leaves, and seeds, contains toxic compounds called alkaloids. The roots of the plant are particularly potent and contain the highest concentration of these toxic alkaloids. The alkaloids in the plant can be fatal if ingested, inhaled, or if they come into contact with the skin. However, there is limited information on the medicinal use of Cicuta douglasii due to its extreme toxicity.
preparations: Cicuta douglasii, also known as tall water hemlock, is a highly toxic plant that has been used in some herbal preparations, despite its dangers. However, due to its extreme toxicity, it is not recommended to use it in any form of herbal remedy. The plant contains a range of toxic compounds, including cicutoxin, which can cause seizures, muscle spasms, and other severe symptoms if ingested. In some traditional and historical contexts, Cicuta douglasii has been used in small, controlled doses to treat conditions such as rheumatism and gout. It's essential to note that any use of this plant is not recommended due to its high toxicity and potential for severe harm.
side_effects: Cicuta douglasii, also known as Douglas' water hemlock, is highly toxic to humans and animals. Ingestion of even small amounts can cause symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. If left untreated, exposure to the plant can lead to seizures, muscle spasms, and respiratory failure. Long-term exposure may result in neurological damage and even death. Cicuta douglasii is often referred to as one of the most toxic plants in North America.
